title: The Tech High Ground: U.S. Leadership in AI and Tech Standards
content:
Offering financing and technical assistance for U.S. technology could help increase global adoption, particularly in regions where China already provides subsidized tech bundles. In this fierce contest, standards and governance matter just as much as the underlying hardware. Standards are the fundamental grammar of global technology, determining how systems interact, how data is governed, and how risks are managed. These critical rules extend from AI safety and data privacy to emerging fields like biotechnology and biomanufacturing. The United States must actively lead in these international standard-setting bodies rather than stepping away.

Washington should prioritize developing standard evaluations to test AI systems before their release, ensuring they work safely in real-world scenarios. The U.S. and its partners must also back strict safeguards for synthetic biology, given its increasing convergence with artificial intelligence. Establishing common screening protocols and incident-reporting norms will prevent dangerous applications from causing unforeseen catastrophes. Critics who argue that a focus on risk and safety hinders U.S. competitiveness against China have the situation completely backward. Ensuring security and trustworthiness will actually accelerate adoption, as uncertainty and unreliability naturally make industries reluctant to deploy new capabilities.

As the United States aims to fortify its high ground in technology, it must also create space for stability in the U.S.-Chinese relationship. This cooperation must be a core pillar of the strategy rather than a mere afterthought. Neither country is going away, and both great powers will ultimately have to learn to coexist and manage shared technological risks. The competition will end in catastrophe if they fail to work together to avoid destabilizing spirals. Therefore, shaping global efforts toward AI security demands both fierce innovation and pragmatic international cooperation.
